hikes.name: Snake Mountain
hikes.description: A favorite for Middlebury students; offers a massive stone foundation at the top with a cliff-side view of Lake Champlain.

hikes.name: Robert Frost Interpretive Trail
hikes.description: A flat, accessible loop featuring poems by Robert Frost on plaques throughout the woods and fields.

hikes.name: Abbey Pond Trail
hikes.description: A steady climb following a stream up to a high-elevation pond and wetland area.

hikes.name: Chipman Hill Loop
hikes.description: Located right in town; part of the TAM, featuring a network of trails with views of the college and village.

hikes.name: TAM: Otter Creek Gorge
hikes.description: Part of the Trail Around Middlebury, this section follows the dramatic limestone gorge and waterfalls.

hikes.name: Mount Moosalamoo
hikes.description: A steep climb in the Moosalamoo National Recreation Area with views of Lake Dunmore.

hikes.name: Silver Lake Trail
hikes.description: Beginning at Branbury State Park, this trail climbs to a secluded, mountain-ringed lake.

hikes.name: Falls of Lana
hikes.description: A short walk to a series of cascading waterfalls that feed into Lake Dunmore.

hikes.name: Skylight Pond (Skyline Lodge)
hikes.description: Follows the Ripton-Goshen road up to a beautiful pond and a Green Mountain Club lodge.

hikes.name: Belden Falls Loop
hikes.description: A scenic walk along the Otter Creek featuring a suspension bridge and views of the dam.

hikes.name: Bristol Ledges
hikes.description: A short, steep hike directly above Bristol village with a bird's-eye view of the town's main street.

hikes.name: Mount Abraham via Battell Trail
hikes.description: The local favorite for summiting the 4,000-footer from the west side.

hikes.name: Sunset Ledge
hikes.description: Starting at the top of Lincoln Gap, a very short hike on the Long Trail with high reward views.

hikes.name: Texas Falls Nature Trail
hikes.description: A lush, forested loop around a unique glacial pothole and waterfall.

hikes.name: Bristol Cliffs Wilderness
hikes.description: A rugged, trailless wilderness area for experienced navigators seeking solitude.

hikes.name: Mount Mansfield (Sunset Ridge)
hikes.description: The classic open-ridge ascent to the highest point in Vermont.

hikes.name: Stowe Pinnacle
hikes.description: Popular steady climb to a rocky summit overlooking the valley.

hikes.name: Moss Glen Falls
hikes.description: Spectacular 85-foot waterfall just minutes from the road.

hikes.name: Sterling Pond
hikes.description: Steep climb to the highest trout pond in the state.

hikes.name: Bingham Falls
hikes.description: Deep swimming holes and a narrow gorge waterfall.

hikes.name: Haselton Trail
hikes.description: Rugged terrain starting near the ski resort and ascending the mountain flank.

hikes.name: Wiessner Woods
hikes.description: A gentle forest walk perfect for families and dogs.

hikes.name: Kimmer Trail
hikes.description: A winding trail popular for both hikers and mountain bikers.

hikes.name: Camel's Hump (Monroe Trail)
hikes.description: Iconic treeless summit with 360-degree views.

hikes.name: Mount Hunger
hikes.description: A steep trek in the Worcester Range with a bald, rocky peak.

hikes.name: Little River Nature Trail
hikes.description: Historic farmstead ruins and gentle forest paths.

hikes.name: Stevenson Brook Trail
hikes.description: Follows an old logging road through Little River State Park.

hikes.name: Cotton Brook Trail
hikes.description: Longer, rolling trail through remote state forest land.

hikes.name: Reservoir Perimeter Trail
hikes.description: Flat trail hugging the shoreline of the reservoir.

hikes.name: Camel's Hump (Burrows Trail)
hikes.description: The shortest, though very popular and rocky, route to the summit.

hikes.name: Lye Brook Falls
hikes.description: A 125-foot waterfall in the Lye Brook Wilderness.

hikes.name: Mount Equinox
hikes.description: Toughest climb in the Taconic Range with views of the Charterhouse.

hikes.name: Prospect Rock
hikes.description: Follows the Long Trail to a cliff overlooking the valley.

hikes.name: Equinox Pond Loop
hikes.description: Serene stroll around the base of the mountain.

hikes.name: Mount Aeolus
hikes.description: Leads to marble caves and historic quarry sites.

hikes.name: Gettysburg Quarry
hikes.description: An abandoned marble quarry with vertical white stone walls.

hikes.name: Owl's Head (Dorset)
hikes.description: Steep hike to a viewpoint overlooking the Dorset valley.

hikes.name: Killington Peak (Bucklin Trail)
hikes.description: Vermont's second-highest peak; very steep final pitch.

hikes.name: Deer Leap Rock
hikes.description: Dramatic cliff overlook above the Sherburne Pass.

hikes.name: Thundering Falls Boardwalk
hikes.description: Wheelchair-accessible path to a major AT waterfall.

hikes.name: Pico Peak
hikes.description: A climb to the neighbor of Killington with a different perspective.

hikes.name: Kent Pond Loop
hikes.description: Quiet shoreline walk on the Appalachian Trail.

hikes.name: Shrewsbury Peak
hikes.description: A more remote peak in the Coolidge State Forest.

hikes.name: Mount Philo
hikes.description: Small mountain, legendary views of the Lake and Adirondacks.

hikes.name: Shelburne Bay Park
hikes.description: Lakeside trails with limestone cliffs.

hikes.name: Niquette Bay State Park
hikes.description: Sandy beaches and views of the Lake Champlain islands.

hikes.name: Red Rocks Park
hikes.description: Public park with high cliffs overlooking the main lake.

hikes.name: Centennial Woods
hikes.description: UVM-managed forest with diverse ecosystems.

hikes.name: LaPlatte Nature Park
hikes.description: Meandering river paths and floodplains.

hikes.name: Shelburne Farms Loop
hikes.description: World-class farm landscape with lake views and grand architecture.

hikes.name: Mount Pisgah
hikes.description: Iconic cliffs overlooking the fjord-like Lake Willoughby.

hikes.name: Mount Hor
hikes.description: View of the Gap from the western side of Willoughby.

hikes.name: Bald Mountain (Westmore)
hikes.description: Restored fire tower with views to Canada and the Whites.

hikes.name: Wheeler Mountain
hikes.description: Unique granite slabs and open rock hiking.

hikes.name: Sentinel Rock
hikes.description: A massive glacial erratic boulder with a pastoral view.

hikes.name: Bluff Mountain
hikes.description: Overlooks the village of Island Pond.

hikes.name: Jay Peak
hikes.description: Rugged northern hike with views deep into Quebec.

hikes.name: Mount Abraham (Long Trail)
hikes.description: Alpine zone at the summit with 360-degree views.

hikes.name: Molly Stark's Balcony
hikes.description: Scenic ledge overlooking the Mad River Valley.

hikes.name: Burnt Rock Mountain
hikes.description: Remote, rocky summit on the Monroe Skyline.

hikes.name: Mad River Path
hikes.description: Community trails connecting the local villages.

hikes.name: Mount Ellen
hikes.description: One of the 4,000 footers, often accessed via the ski area.

hikes.name: Harmon Hill
hikes.description: Steep rock stairs leading to a high, open meadow.

hikes.name: Bald Mountain (Bennington)
hikes.description: View of the Bennington Battle Monument.

hikes.name: Little Rock Pond
hikes.description: Crystal clear pond on the Long Trail.

hikes.name: Stratton Mountain
hikes.description: Fire tower hike where the AT and LT were conceived.

hikes.name: White Rocks Ice Beds
hikes.description: Unique microclimate with ice in the talus slopes.

hikes.name: Bromley Mountain
hikes.description: Gradual climb to a grassy summit with great views.

hikes.name: Mount Olga
hikes.description: Short fire tower loop in Molly Stark State Park.

hikes.name: Haystack Mountain
hikes.description: View overlooking the Deerfield Valley and Reservoir.

hikes.name: Mount Ascutney
hikes.description: Isolated monadnock with waterfalls and a stone tower.

hikes.name: Mount Tom
hikes.description: Carriage roads in the National Historical Park.

hikes.name: The Pogue
hikes.description: Hidden high-elevation pond in Woodstock.

hikes.name: Gile Mountain
hikes.description: Fire tower with views of the Connecticut River valley.

hikes.name: Mount Peg
hikes.description: Local Woodstock hill with a view of the village.
